Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The process begins with Receive order and then must have Check inventory before any decisions about payment or fulfillment If inventory is sufficient the sequence should be Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order If inventory is insufficient it should be Check inventory Cancel order Close order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key constraints from the description Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation Close order should be the final activity in all scenarios

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order This follows the sufficient inventory path exactly No violations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Receive order Check inventory Package items Authorize payment Ship order Close order Here Package items comes before Authorize payment According to the description Package items should only occur after authorization This is a violation because payment authorization should happen before packaging

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Cancel order Ship order Close order Authorize payment occurs then Cancel order then Ship order But Ship order should never occur after a cancellation Also if canceled the process should go directly to Cancel order then Close order not have Ship order after cancellation This violates the rule that Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after cancellation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order This follows the insufficient inventory path correctly No violations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Ship order Close order Missing Package items activity According to the description after authorization items should be packaged before shipping So this is a violation because packaging is skipped

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Now lets double check for any ambiguities The description says Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping In Case 5 shipping happens without packaging which is clearly a violation In Case 2 packaging before authorization is also a violation In Case 3 shipping after cancellation is a violation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the violating cases are Case 2 Case 3 and Case 5 Case 1 and Case 4 are correct
